curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 11 units, 11 chapters, 33 topics, 15 subtopics, and 19 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Derive and apply the standard equation of a circle.
Apply principles to solve linear inequalities.
Analyze sequences and series including arithmetic and geometric progressions.
Apply coordinate geometry principles to solve distance and intersection problems.
Calculate the distance between two points in a three-dimensional space.
Define and calculate derivatives as rates of change.
Apply axiomatic probability to determine the likelihood of events.
Represent sets using various methods and perform operations like union, intersection, and complementation.
Analyze relations and functions including domain, range, and composition of functions.
Apply trigonometric identities and solve equations involving general solutions.
Apply the Principle of Mathematical Induction to prove statements about natural numbers.
Solve quadratic equations in the complex number system using polar representation.

units.description: Complex Numbers and Quadratic Equations, Linear Inequalities, Permutations and Combinations, Binomial Theorem, Sequence and Series, Complex Numbers, Quadratic Equations, Argand Plane
Straight Lines, Slope, Equation of Line

Sections of a cone: circles, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola, a point, a straight line and a pair of intersecting lines as a degenerated case of a conic section., Standard equations and simple properties of parabola, ellipse and hyperbola., Standard equation of a circle., Conic Sections, Circle, Ellipse, Parabola, Hyperbola
topics: Sections of a cone: circles, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola, a point, a straight line and a pair of intersecting lines as a degenerated case of a conic section.
Standard equations and simple properties of parabola, ellipse and hyperbola.

topics: Coordinate axes and coordinate planes in three dimensions.

units.description: Limits and Derivatives, Limit, Derivative, Rate of Change
Statistics, Probability, Measures of Dispersion (Range, Mean deviation, Variance, Standard Deviation), Dispersion, Standard Deviation, Axiomatic Probability

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: No chapter-wise weightage. Care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
internal_assessment: Periodic Tests (Best 2 out of 3 tests conducted): 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: No chapter-wise weightage. Care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
internal_assessment: Periodic Tests (Best 2 out of 3 tests conducted): 10 Marks

competency_groups.title: Conic Sections Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding conic sections and their standard equations.
competencies: Identify different types of conic sections including circles, ellipses, parabolas, hyperbolas, and degenerate cases.
Derive and apply the standard equation of a circle.
Apply properties to solve problems involving parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas.

competency_groups.title: Algebra Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Mastery of complex numbers, inequalities, combinatorics, and series.
competencies: Solve quadratic equations in the complex number system using algebraic properties.
Apply principles to solve linear inequalities.
Calculate permutations and combinations for problem-solving scenarios.
Utilize the Binomial Theorem for expansions.
Analyze sequences and series including arithmetic and geometric progressions.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Application of linear equations and geometric properties.
competencies: Determine the slope and equation of straight lines in various forms.
Apply coordinate geometry principles to solve distance and intersection problems.

competency_groups.title: Three-dimensional Geometry Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Spatial reasoning and coordinate calculations in 3D.
competencies: Identify coordinate axes, planes, and points in three dimensions.
Calculate the distance between two points in a three-dimensional space.

competency_groups.title: Calculus Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Foundational concepts of limits and derivatives.
competencies: Understand the intuitive concept of limits for various functions.
Define and calculate derivatives as rates of change.

competency_groups.title: Statistics and Probability Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Data analysis and probabilistic reasoning.
competencies: Calculate measures of dispersion including range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation.
Apply axiomatic probability to determine the likelihood of events.

competency_groups.title: Sets and Functions Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Foundational set theory, function properties, and trigonometry.
competencies: Represent sets using various methods and perform operations like union, intersection, and complementation.
Analyze relations and functions including domain, range, and composition of functions.
Apply trigonometric identities and solve equations involving general solutions.

competency_groups.title: Algebra Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Advanced algebraic techniques including induction, complex numbers, and series.
competencies: Apply the Principle of Mathematical Induction to prove statements about natural numbers.
Solve quadratic equations in the complex number system using polar representation.
Graphically solve linear inequalities in two variables.
Determine general and middle terms in binomial expansions.
Calculate sums of special series involving powers of integers.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Equations of lines and spatial geometry basics.
competencies: Derive equations of straight lines in normal form, general form, and other standard forms.
Apply the section formula to find coordinates dividing a line segment.

competency_groups.title: Calculus Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Introduction to differentiation and limits.
competencies: Apply the chain rule for derivatives of composite functions.

competency_groups.title: Statistics and Probability Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Fundamental probability concepts.
competencies: Define random experiments, outcomes, and sample spaces using set representation.

Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: No chapter-wise weightage. Care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
internal_assessment: Periodic Tests (Best 2 out of 3 tests conducted): 10 Marks

learning_outcomes.topic: Identify different types of conic sections including circles, ellipses, parabolas, hyperbolas, and degenerate cases.
outcomes: Derive and apply the standard equation of a circle.

learning_outcomes.topic: Apply properties to solve problems involving parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas.
Solve quadratic equations in the complex number system using algebraic properties.
outcomes: Apply principles to solve linear inequalities.

learning_outcomes.topic: Calculate permutations and combinations for problem-solving scenarios.
Utilize the Binomial Theorem for expansions.
outcomes: Analyze sequences and series including arithmetic and geometric progressions.

learning_outcomes.topic: Determine the slope and equation of straight lines in various forms.
outcomes: Apply coordinate geometry principles to solve distance and intersection problems.

learning_outcomes.topic: Identify coordinate axes, planes, and points in three dimensions.
outcomes: Calculate the distance between two points in a three-dimensional space.

learning_outcomes.topic: Understand the intuitive concept of limits for various functions.
outcomes: Define and calculate derivatives as rates of change.

learning_outcomes.topic: Calculate measures of dispersion including range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation.
outcomes: Apply axiomatic probability to determine the likelihood of events.

learning_outcomes.topic: Foundational set theory, function properties, and trigonometry
outcomes: Represent sets using various methods and perform operations like union, intersection, and complementation.
Analyze relations and functions including domain, range, and composition of functions.
Apply trigonometric identities and solve equations involving general solutions.

learning_outcomes.topic: Advanced algebraic techniques including induction, complex numbers, and series
outcomes: Apply the Principle of Mathematical Induction to prove statements about natural numbers.
Solve quadratic equations in the complex number system using polar representation.
Graphically solve linear inequalities in two variables.
Determine general and middle terms in binomial expansions.
Calculate sums of special series involving powers of integers.

learning_outcomes.topic: Equations of lines and spatial geometry basics
outcomes: Derive equations of straight lines in normal form, general form, and other standard forms.
Apply the section formula to find coordinates dividing a line segment.

learning_outcomes.topic: Introduction to differentiation and limits
outcomes: Apply the chain rule for derivatives of composite functions.

learning_outcomes.topic: Fundamental probability concepts
outcomes: Define random experiments, outcomes, and sample spaces using set representation.
